summary_oneline: Redis-backed partial form answer caching with TTL, session isolation, and real-time per-field validation

localization.role: Field model — field_id is partial-save key
Integration point for merging cached partials

claims.claim: FormCache pattern is the correct template for PartialSaveStore
Redis key parrot:partial:{form_id}:{session_id} provides correct isolation
session_id available via request['session']['id'] on all auth routes
FormValidator.validate_field() enables per-field validation on save
Feature is purely additive — no existing code modified beyond wiring
1-hour TTL aligns with existing FormCache default (3600s)
New values merge over cached using dict update
Nested fields (GROUP/ARRAY) may need special key handling

unknowns.question: Should submit endpoint auto-merge cached partials?
unknowns.user_answer: Both supported — query param ?merge_partials=true controls behavior. Default: false.

unknowns.question: Should partial saves include per-field validation?
unknowns.user_answer: Validate on save — return per-field errors immediately for real-time UX.

recommended_next_command.rationale: High-confidence localization, well-bounded scope, all unknowns resolved. Ready for formal specification.
